title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for request smuggling, response splitting, parser differentials, and downgrades and requires the smallest strict multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 8319 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Downgrade Consistency Gate.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test http/2 or http/3 to http/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Downgrade Consistency Gate, keep Downgrade Consistency Gate connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Downgrade Consistency Gate, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A safe HTTP/2 front end guarantees the translated HTTP/1.1 backend request cannot be ambiguous.
